content.problemStatement:
You are given a strictly increasing sequence of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Your task is to compress this sequence.

The compressed form of this sequence is a sequence of ranges separated by commas (characters ','). A range is either an integer or a pair of integers separated by three dots (the string `"..."`). When each range `a...b` in the compressed form is decompressed into the subsequence (a,a+1,…,b)(a, a+1, \ldots, b)(a,a+1,…,b), we should obtain the (comma-separated) sequence AAA again.

For each maximal contiguous subsequence (a,a+1,…,b)(a, a+1, \ldots, b)(a,a+1,…,b) of AAA such that b≥a+2b \ge a+2b≥a+2, the compressed form of AAA must contain the range `a...b`; if b≤a+1b \le a+1b≤a+1, such a sequence should not be compressed into a range. A contiguous subsequence is maximal if it cannot be extended by at least one element of AAA next to it. It can be proved that the compressed form of any sequence is unique (i.e. well-defined).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one string ― the compressed form of the given sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

(1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3) is a contiguous subsequence with length 333, so it is replaced by the string "1...3"
(5,6)(5, 6)(5,6) is a contiguous subsequence, but only with length 222, so it is not compressed into a range
(8,9,10,11,12)(8, 9, 10, 11, 12)(8,9,10,11,12) is a contiguous subsequence with length 555, so it is replaced by "8...12"
the elements 151515, 171717 are unaffected
